assignment: Initialization idempotency: verify collab bus + state init do not truncate ledgers; identify restart risks.
summary: Collab bus ledgers are created idempotently with append-mode touch (no truncation). Run-level state.json is written via atomic tmp+replace. However, per-commander telemetry ledgers (child-agents.jsonl and swarm-state.json) are initialized with write/truncate, so re-running the launcher on an existing run_id (or reinitializing commander progress dirs) can destroy launch-proof history.

findings.claim: Metaswarm collab ledgers are created/initialized without truncation.

evidence.why_it_matters: open(..., "a") creates the file if missing and preserves any existing content.

evidence.snippet: Ledgers are non-empty in this run (e.g., reviews.jsonl has 18 lines; consensus.jsonl 8; broadcasts.jsonl 7).
evidence.why_it_matters: Run artifacts show append-only JSONL content with no header, consistent with protocol guidance.

evidence.snippet: Protocol requires append-only JSONL and explicitly forbids truncation during a run.

findings.claim: Run-level state.json updates are atomic (tmp file then replace), but may overwrite specific fields on restart.

evidence.snippet: write_json_atomic() writes to <path>.tmp then os.replace(); run_state loaded if present then written atomically.
evidence.why_it_matters: Avoids partial writes/corruption if process crashes mid-write.
On restart with same run_id, state.json is rewritten (atomic) and will force these fields regardless of previous values.

findings.claim: Per-commander telemetry files are initialized with truncate semantics, which is not restart-idempotent.

evidence.why_it_matters: If stampede.sh is re-run for the same RUN_ID (or progress_dir reused), existing launch-proof history is wiped.

evidence.snippet: partial_reason explicitly cites: child-agents.jsonl was truncated/restarted during concurrent child writes.
evidence.why_it_matters: Run artifacts already observed/recorded this as a real failure mode impacting success/partial gating.

restart_risks.impact: Mixes old collab history with new protocol/state; and can wipe per-commander telemetry while preserving collab ledgers, yielding inconsistent proof.

evidence.snippet: If tmux session exists, it is killed for fresh launch.

restart_risks.impact: Even with O_APPEND, multi-process text writes can interleave if a record is split across syscalls/buffers; readers must tolerate malformed trailing lines per protocol.

evidence.snippet: Readers must tolerate malformed trailing lines; open ledgers in append mode.

restart_risks.impact: Low; only impacts state.json on teardown, but could corrupt if interrupted mid-write.

recommendations: Keep collab ledgers append-only (current behavior OK).
Make per-commander telemetry init idempotent: create-if-missing (append-mode touch) rather than truncate when RUN_ID already exists.
Default to refusing duplicate RUN_ID unless an explicit --force or --archive-old is provided; otherwise restart behavior can destroy/merge evidence.
If collab ledgers must be fresh per launch, archive/rename old ledgers rather than truncating in place (preserves audit trail).
